data.title: Actium Capital Lifts Stake in Altri with 0.439% Share Purchase
data.description:

  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Altri SGPS ( (GB:0DJV) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Altri SGPS disclosed that Actium Capital, S.A., a related party under director transaction rules, acquired 900,462 Altri shares on the Euronext Lisbon market over three days in late May 2026. The purchases, executed at prices around €4.88 to €4.92 per share, represent approximately 0.439% of Altri’s share capital and voting rights, slightly increasing Actium Capital’s stake and signaling continued investor confidence in the company’s equity.

The transaction notification, made under article 16 of the Portuguese Securities Code, underscores Altri’s compliance with transparency requirements on director and related-party dealings. While the stake increase is modest in percentage terms, it marginally concentrates ownership and may be monitored by shareholders and market participants as an indicator of insider sentiment toward Altri’s prospects.

More about Altri SGPS

Altri SGPS is a Portuguese listed holding company with shares traded on Euronext Lisbon, operating in the industrial and financial investment space through stakes in affiliated businesses. The group is subject to Portuguese securities market regulations governing disclosures of qualifying holdings and transactions by directors and related parties.

data.title: Argosy Property Earnings Call: Stability Amid Headwinds
data.description:
Argosy Property ((NZ:ARG)) has held its Q4 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Argosy Property’s latest earnings call painted a cautiously balanced picture for investors. Management highlighted steady growth in income, stronger interest cover, and a healthier balance sheet, yet repeatedly pointed to macro uncertainty, rising costs, and stubborn vacancies that will weigh on near‑term momentum. The overall tone was pragmatic rather than upbeat, with risk management clearly taking priority over aggressive growth.

Revenue and Net Property Income Growth

Argosy reported gross property income of $137.5 million, up 3.6% year on year, with net property income rising 3.3% to $120.8 million. This modest but consistent top‑line growth suggests the core portfolio is still performing despite softer leasing markets and higher vacancy in some assets.

Distributable Income and Per‑Share Metrics

Gross distributable income climbed 9.8% to $70.4 million, while net distributable income per share rose 7.1% to $0.0705. The per‑share uplift indicates that earnings growth is feeding through to shareholders, even as the dividend remains flat, providing some buffer against inflation eroding real returns.

Funds From Operations Improvement

Funds from operations increased 8.3% to $59.1 million, with FFO per share improving from $0.0643 to $0.0685. This points to healthier recurring cash earnings from the underlying portfolio, strengthening Argosy’s ability to sustain payouts and absorb shocks in a more volatile operating environment.

Portfolio Valuation and Revaluation Gains

The investment property portfolio grew by $94 million to $2.2 billion, including a $4.4 million gain on two held‑for‑sale assets. These valuation gains reflect both asset quality and market resilience, giving Argosy more balance sheet flexibility at a time when capital recycling is central to its strategy.

Balance Sheet Strength and Interest Rate Management

Debt to total assets stood at 37.2% at year‑end and has eased to just over 36% post‑settlements, while the weighted average cost of debt fell to 4.6% from 5.1%. Interest cover improved to 2.7 times and fixed‑rate cover rose to 74%, underscoring a deliberate move to de‑risk funding costs and protect cash flow.

Acquisitions and Development Completions

The company completed the acquisition of 291 East Tamaki Road and finished key capital works at 224 Neilson Street (Warehouse B) and the Mt Richmond development. Capital expenditure was tightly focused on these projects, indicating a shift from expansion toward optimizing and finishing existing commitments.

Sustainability Recognition and Green Demand

Argosy’s sustainability push is gaining traction, with 224 Neilson Street winning industrial gold and overall sustainability awards and Mt Richmond achieving a 6 Green Star rating. Management noted strong enquiry for 5–6 star green industrial buildings, suggesting that environmental performance is becoming a genuine leasing advantage.

Dividend Maintained and Policy Reset

The full‑year dividend was held at $0.0665 per share, with the fourth‑quarter distribution at $0.016625. The board shifted policy to target 80%–95% of FFO and suspended the dividend reinvestment plan, signalling a more conservative, cash‑focused stance aimed at making dividends sustainable rather than simply maximizing payout.

Geopolitical Uncertainty and Imported Inflation

Management highlighted geopolitical risks, including conflict‑linked pressures that reintroduce nonproductive imported inflation into the local economy. They warned of possible market disruption and a prolonged period of uncertainty that could extend beyond a year, shaping a more defensive outlook for capital deployment.

Higher Vacancy and Lease Conversion Delays

Vacancy levels are higher than Argosy would like and leasing conversion times have lengthened by more than a month. A critical lease at 224 Neilson Street remains conditional and, if secured, would now commence on 1 March next year rather than July, leaving about one and a half years of vacancy exposure on a significant asset.

Development Activity to Be Reined In

The company expects minimal development activity in coming periods due to anticipated 10%–15% construction cost inflation driven by oil, transport, and materials. With discretionary spending constrained, Argosy is prioritizing risk control and returns on existing assets over speculative new projects.

Rising Operating Costs and Expense Pressures

Net property expenses increased by $0.9 million as nonrecoverable rates in Wellington and vacancy‑related operating costs outweighed insurance savings. Maintenance costs rose by $1.4 million, reflecting higher spending on tenant fit‑outs and HVAC replacements, which will need to be offset by rent growth and tighter cost discipline.

Flat Dividend Track Record and Dilution Concerns

The dividend has been stuck at $0.0665 per share for five years, prompting shareholder questions about real income erosion against consumer price inflation. The suspension of the DRP removes a low‑friction reinvestment option, sharpening investor focus on whether earnings growth can eventually translate into higher cash returns.

Leasing Market Pressures in Industrial and Retail

Industrial leasing remains sluggish, with oversupply in some pockets and incentives drifting toward roughly 12% to secure deals. Large‑format retail is expected to struggle outside prime locations such as Albany, making asset selection and tenant quality more critical for maintaining occupancy and rental resilience.

Tenant and Asset‑Specific Vacancy Challenges

Vacancy at 101 Carlton Gore Road nearly doubled following a lease roll‑out, highlighting sensitivity in office‑style assets. Upcoming expiries, including the warehouse at 17 Mayo Road and other leases, will require active management to limit revenue leakage and avoid a further drag on occupancy metrics.

FFO Volatility and Dividend Basis Change

Argosy shifted its dividend reference from FO to FFO, arguing that FO is distorted by volatile adjustments such as amortization, leasing incentives, and below‑the‑line items. Management also cited one‑off tax and deduction timing issues, positioning FFO as a more stable measure for setting predictable distributions.

Forward‑Looking Guidance and Outlook

Guidance for FY27 keeps the dividend flat at $0.0665 per share under the new 80%–95% FFO payout policy, with the DRP still suspended. Argosy expects minimal development, rising construction costs of 10%–15%, and, if the Neilson Street lease completes, occupancy improving to about 97.2% with a WALE near 5.3 years, underpinned by a stronger, more hedged balance sheet.

Argosy’s earnings call underscored a mix of solid operating gains and genuine headwinds, leaving the near‑term story one of disciplined defense rather than rapid growth. Income and valuations are moving in the right direction and the balance sheet is better protected, but higher vacancies, cost pressures, and a flat dividend mean investors will need patience as management prioritizes stability over expansion.

data.title: SentinelOne Earnings Call Highlights Profitable Growth
data.description:
Sentinelone, Inc. ((S)) has held its Q1 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

SentinelOne’s latest earnings call struck a broadly upbeat tone, as management highlighted accelerating recurring revenue, record net new ARR, surging demand for its AI‑driven security tools, and rapidly improving profitability. While they acknowledged restructuring moves, seasonal billing quirks, and macro uncertainty, the decision to raise profit guidance underscored confidence in balancing growth with margin expansion.

Revenue Growth

Revenue climbed 21% year over year to $277.0 million in Q1 FY27, setting what executives called a solid foundation for the year. Management reaffirmed full‑year revenue guidance of $1.195–$1.205 billion, implying roughly 20% growth at the midpoint and signaling no change in top‑line ambitions.

ARR Acceleration and Record Net New ARR

Total annual recurring revenue accelerated 23% year over year, as the company added a record $44 million in net new ARR during the quarter. That figure represented a 55% increase from last year and marked the fourth consecutive quarter of positive net new ARR growth, reinforcing momentum in the subscription base.

AI Security Traction

AI security continued to be a standout growth driver, with AI‑related ARR nearly doubling again in Q1 according to management. The company spotlighted its Prompt Security offering as a competitive differentiator, winning standalone deals and displacing incumbent vendors in a crowded cybersecurity market.

Platform Mix and Cross‑Sell Momentum

For the first time, non‑endpoint solutions approached about half of total ARR, underscoring SentinelOne’s evolution into a broader security platform. ARR per customer hit a new high while the $100,000‑plus ARR cohort grew 17% year over year, highlighting successful upmarket moves and deeper multi‑product adoption.

International Expansion

International operations remained a growth engine, with revenue rising 25% year over year and reaching 39% of total sales. This expanding global mix suggests SentinelOne is gaining traction beyond its core U.S. base, adding geographic diversification to its growth story.

Profitability Improvement

Profitability took a notable step forward as operating margin improved to 4% in Q1, a roughly 550 basis point gain from a year earlier. Management raised full‑year FY27 operating income guidance to $115–$125 million, implying about a 10% margin at the midpoint and a 650 basis point improvement versus last year.

Earnings and Cash Profile

Non‑GAAP earnings power improved sharply, with EPS up about 83% year over year to $0.40 in Q1. The company reported a trailing‑12‑month adjusted free cash flow margin of 6.5% and ended the quarter with $812 million in cash, cash equivalents, and investments, and no debt on the balance sheet.

Contract Visibility and Bookings Signals

Contract visibility strengthened as remaining performance obligations climbed roughly 30% year over year to a record $1.5 billion. The SentinelOne Flex program surpassed $200 million in total contract value in under a year, aided by multiple large seven‑ and eight‑figure deals and multi‑year customer commitments.

Product and Partner Momentum

Product innovation and partnerships featured prominently, including launches like Singularity AI red teaming, Purple AI auto investigations and Wayfinder Frontier AI services. The company also pointed to expanded alliances with major cloud and security partners and highlighted notable customer wins across government, luxury retail, and cloud security use cases.

Workforce Reduction and Restructuring Charge

Amid the growth, SentinelOne announced an approximately 8% workforce reduction, tied to a broader efficiency push. Management expects a one‑time restructuring charge of about $25 million in Q2, excluded from non‑GAAP results, and is targeting around $45 million in annualized cost savings from these actions.

Conservative Revenue Linearity Despite Strong ARR

Despite the surge in ARR and bookings, the company maintained existing revenue guidance, citing the timing of larger deals and some back‑end loading. This cautious stance suggests management is wary of near‑term revenue recognition risk even as underlying subscription momentum remains strong.

Billings and Deferred Revenue Variability

Analysts probed variability in deferred revenue and billings, which appeared softer than headline ARR might suggest. The CFO attributed this to invoicing timing and seasonality, flagging billings as an area where quarter‑to‑quarter swings may not always align neatly with underlying demand.

Macro and Geopolitical Uncertainty

The company acknowledged that evolving macroeconomic and geopolitical conditions could influence deal timing and sales cycles. While no specific slowdown was called out, management noted that such external factors remain a potential headwind to near‑term growth cadence.

Concentration of Expansion in Top Cohorts

Improving dollar‑based net retention was most evident among customers generating more than $100,000 of ARR, underscoring a heavy reliance on large accounts for expansion. This pattern implies smaller cohorts are still ramping and that future NRR gains may depend on broadening adoption beyond the current top tier.

Forward‑Looking Guidance and Outlook

Looking ahead, SentinelOne reiterated FY27 revenue guidance of $1.195–$1.205 billion and Q2 revenue of $289–$291 million, both implying about 20% growth. The company guided FY27 operating income to $115–$125 million and non‑GAAP EPS of roughly $0.32–$0.38, while expecting continued positive net new ARR growth and emphasizing its sizeable cash position and lack of debt.

SentinelOne’s earnings call painted the picture of a security vendor transitioning from high‑growth disruptor to increasingly profitable platform player. Strong ARR gains, deepening AI and international traction, and rising margins were tempered by restructuring and billing timing risks, leaving investors to weigh robust fundamentals against execution and macro uncertainties.

data.title: Anvi Global Holdings Delays Annual 10-K Filing
data.description:
Anvi Global Holdings ( (ANVI) ) has released a notification of late filing.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Anvi Global Holdings, Inc. has filed a Form 12b-25, notifying investors of a delayed Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the fiscal year ended February 28, 2026. The company confirms it is behind schedule on its annual report but remains current on all other required SEC filings over the past 12 months.

Management attributes the delay to difficulties in obtaining and compiling certain information needed for the Form 10-K (Yearly Report). The company says these issues could not be resolved without unreasonable effort and expense, and does not cite auditor changes, control failures, or special committee reviews as causes.

Anvi Global Holdings, Inc. states that it intends to file the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) no later than the 15th calendar day after the original due date. This timeline aligns with the standard extension window allowed under SEC Rule 12b-25 for late annual reports.

The company does not expect any significant change in its results of operations compared with the prior fiscal year. No preliminary figures were provided, and investors should treat any expectations about timing and future results as forward-looking and therefore subject to change.

Anvi Global Holdings, Inc. emphasizes that it is working to complete the required disclosures and restore full compliance with SEC reporting deadlines. The notification is signed by Chief Executive Officer Rama Mohan R. Busa on May 29, 2026, signaling executive-level oversight of the delayed filing and the planned catch-up schedule.

More about Anvi Global Holdings

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: $6M

Scienture Holdings ( (SCNX) ) has shared an announcement.

On May 26, 2026, Scienture Holdings, Inc. announced that Chief Financial Officer Eric Sherb resigned for personal reasons, with the company emphasizing that his departure did not stem from any dispute with management or the board. The move marks a leadership transition in the finance function but does not suggest underlying governance or operational conflict.

Effective the same day, the company appointed Co-Chief Executive Officer and President Dr. Narasimhan Mani as Interim CFO, drawing on his oversight of Scienture’s financial operations since May 2025. With more than 25 years of senior experience across major pharmaceutical companies and specialty pharma ventures, as well as an MBA in finance, Dr. Mani’s appointment is intended to provide continuity and reinforce financial and strategic oversight during the transition.

data.title: Cencora Names New CFO and Reaffirms 2026 Outlook
data.description:
Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

An announcement from Cencora ( (COR) ) is now available.

On May 27, 2026, Cencora announced the appointment of veteran finance executive Eva C. Boratto as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, effective June 29, 2026, succeeding retiring CFO James F. Cleary, who will remain in an advisory role through year-end to ensure continuity. Boratto brings extensive healthcare and consumer-sector experience from senior roles at Bath & Body Works, CVS Health and Merck, as well as audit committee chair positions at Mars and UPS, bolstering Cencora’s financial leadership bench as it pursues its pharmaceutical-centric growth strategy.

Alongside the leadership change, Cencora reaffirmed its adjusted diluted earnings per share guidance of $17.70 to $17.90 for fiscal 2026—updated on May 21, 2026 following opportunistic share repurchases—and reiterated long-term targets for adjusted operating income growth of 7% to 10% and adjusted EPS growth of 10% to 14%. The reaffirmed outlook signals management’s confidence in the company’s current trajectory and suggests that the CFO transition is not expected to disrupt its financial performance or long-term value-creation plans for shareholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (COR) stock is a Buy with a $331.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Cencora stock, see the COR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on COR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, COR is a Neutral.

The score reflects solid operational performance and a supportive earnings outlook (raised EPS/operating income guidance and continued free-cash-flow expectations), offset by meaningful balance-sheet leverage risk and deteriorated recent free cash flow. Technicals are a notable drag with a clear downtrend and weak momentum, while valuation is a relative positive due to the low P/E.

To see Spark’s full report on COR stock, click here.

More about Cencora

Cencora is a leading global pharmaceutical solutions organization that partners with drug innovators and care providers to facilitate and optimize market access to therapies and ensure secure, reliable delivery of pharmaceuticals and healthcare products for people and animals. The company is ranked No. 10 on the Fortune 500 and No. 18 on the Global Fortune 500, generating more than $300 billion in annual revenue from its pharmaceutical-centric operations.

Operating at the center of the global pharmaceutical supply chain, Cencora focuses on supporting positive health outcomes through logistics, distribution and related solutions across the healthcare ecosystem. Its business model positions it as a critical intermediary between manufacturers and providers, underpinning its scale-driven margins and its ability to pursue steady, long-term earnings growth.

data.title: Kennedy-Wilson Subsidiary Completes $1.8 Billion Note Offering
data.description:
Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

An announcement from Kennedy-Wilson ( (KW) ) is now available.

On May 29, 2026, Kennedy-Wilson, Inc., a wholly owned subsidiary of Kennedy-Wilson Holdings, completed a $1.8 billion private offering of senior notes, split between $1.1 billion of 7.000% notes due 2031 and $700 million of 7.250% notes due 2033, sold to qualified institutional buyers and certain non-U.S. investors. The notes, which carry customary covenants and optional redemption features, have interest payable semiannually starting December 1, 2026.

The gross proceeds have been placed in escrow pending the consummation of a previously announced merger involving a management-led consortium and Fairfax Financial Holdings, with the funds expected to be used to redeem existing notes, fund an offer for other outstanding notes and repay credit facility debt if the transaction closes. If the merger is not completed by November 16, 2026, the notes will be subject to a special mandatory redemption at par plus accrued interest, with Fairfax committed to cover any shortfall in the escrow account, while post-closing the notes are to become unsecured obligations guaranteed by the company and certain subsidiaries.

The most recent analyst rating on (KW) stock is a Sell with a $11.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Kennedy-Wilson stock, see the KW Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on KW Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, KW is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by high leverage and inconsistent cash flow (including negative TTM free cash flow) despite improving recent profitability. Technicals are supportive with price above key moving averages and positive MACD, and valuation is reasonable with a solid dividend yield. Corporate events (go-private deal and related financing) provide some support but also highlight balance-sheet risk.

Kennedy-Wilson Holdings, Inc. is a global real estate investment company that operates through subsidiaries including Kennedy-Wilson, Inc. The business focuses on owning, operating and financing real estate assets, using capital markets and debt issuance to support portfolio management and corporate transactions across its investment platform.
